title: Pagination, Filtering, Links, Rate Limits, Versioning, and Resumable Work · Browser and Node Client… · Compiler Case: Query Filter Encoding
summary: An infinite-scroll accessibility view traverses cursor links, rate limits, filter encodings, resumable state, and focus restoration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts query filter encoding increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch resumable rate-aware API traversal. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Encode repeated parameters, arrays, dates, sorting, and filters according to the API contract without manual concatenation. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
